steps.title: Compute distance for walking and running
steps.content: Distance equals rate times time. Walking gives $$3 \cdot w = 3w$$ miles and running gives $$5 \cdot r = 5r$$ miles.

steps.title: Write the total-distance equation
steps.content:
The combined total is 14 miles, so $$3w + 5r = 14.$$
  • A) $3w+5r=14$ — matches the distance equation.
  • B) $\frac{1}{3}w+\frac{1}{5}r=14$ — uses reciprocals of the rates, which would give time, not distance.
  • C) $\frac{1}{3}w+\frac{1}{5}r=112$ — wrong coefficients and wrong total.
  • D) $3w+5r=112$ — correct coefficients but wrong total (the total is 14, not 112).
That matches choice A — A is correct.
